name: StatsBomb Data API
what_it_does: Advanced football analytics platform providing event-level and tracking data for deep tactical analysis, with free open data for academic use and commercial data via contract.
use_cases: Analyzing player movement and tactical patterns using event-level tracking data
Computing advanced football metrics like xG, xA, and pressure events
Building football scouting and recruitment analysis pipelines
Academic and research projects using the free open dataset
Generating deep match reports and post-game analysis for media
not_for: Live real-time data — StatsBomb data is post-match and not suitable for in-game feeds
Casual or quick lookup use cases — data is granular and requires significant processing
Commercial use without a paid contract — open data license restricts commercial applications
best_when: An agent or analytics pipeline needs the deepest available event-level football data, with spatial coordinates and detailed action classification, for tactical or research purposes.
avoid_when: You need live scores, real-time odds, or basic league standings — or you need commercial rights without a StatsBomb contract.

auth.notes: Open data is publicly accessible via GitHub with no auth. Commercial API access uses API key credentials provided after contract signing. Open data Python/R clients require no auth.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free open dataset available on GitHub: select competitions including Women's World Cup, FA WSL, NWSL, UCL, La Liga; academic/non-commercial use only
pricing.paid_tiers: Commercial data access requires contract; pricing not public — targeted at professional clubs, broadcasters, and betting firms
pricing.notes: The open-data GitHub repository is a genuine and substantial free resource for researchers and developers. Commercial access requires direct engagement with StatsBomb.

performance.rate_limits: Open data via GitHub: no rate limits beyond GitHub API limits. Commercial API rate limits per contract.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Python/R SDKs raise exceptions with readable messages. Raw JSON from open data has no error layer — agents must validate schema themselves.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: All data access is read-only; open data is static files on GitHub
known_agent_gotchas: Data is structured as nested JSON with complex event schemas requiring significant parsing
Open data uses static GitHub file downloads — not a streaming or query API
Competition and match IDs must be discovered by traversing index files, not a search API
Commercial API is entirely separate from open data with different access patterns
Event data volume per match is very large (thousands of events) — agents must handle bulk data

security_score_components.security_notes: Open data requires no credentials — no security concerns for that path. Commercial API key handling follows standard patterns. Python/R SDKs are well-maintained open source with visible dependency hygiene.
